description: The provided image is a software interface button displaying the text "Check for updates", with a graphic consisting of a red bookmark icon enclosed in a half-blue, half-yellow incomplete circular ring on a light gray square background. No content related to the research on Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel, such as microstructure characterization images, performance test curves, phase structure diagrams, or co-precipitation mechanism schematics mentioned in the paper scope, is visible in this image, so no material science-related scientific information can be obtained from the visual content of this figure.

This figure consists of one high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM) image and two corresponding selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns for aged Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel, which was treated by 1000 °C solution treatment for 1 h followed by 550 °C aging for 3 h. The HRTEM image with 5 nm scale bar marks two selected regions: the yellow box for γ (austenite) + κ-carbide region, and the red box for γ + Cu-rich phase region. The two SAED patterns with 2 1/nm scale bar are both collected along the [011̅] zone axis, with characteristic crystal planes calibrated for each region. SAED results confirm the Cu-rich phase has BCC structure, both κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase are coherent with the austenitic matrix, supporting the cube-on-cube orientation relationship among the three phases and validating the co-precipitation feature of the two precipitates in austenite matrix.

This figure presents microstructural characterization of aged Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu (3Cu) austenitic low-density steel. Subfigure (a) is a low-magnification bright-field TEM image with 50 nm scale bar, showing nanoscale precipitates distributed in the austenitic matrix. Subfigure (b) is EDS elemental mapping of the same region with 50 nm scale bar, presenting the distribution of Fe, Mn, Al and Cu, confirming that κ-carbide (white dashed box zone) is rich in Fe, Mn, Al and depleted in Cu, while Cu-rich phase (magenta line zone) is rich in Cu and depleted in matrix elements Fe, Mn, Al. Subfigure (c) is a HRTEM image with 5 nm scale bar, showing the morphology of irregular oval-shaped Cu-rich phase. Subfigure (d) is a HRTEM image with 5 nm scale bar, showing bar-shaped Cu-rich phase, with inset measuring lattice fringe spacing d=0.182 nm corresponding to Cu crystal plane. This figure verifies the elemental partitioning during co-precipitation of κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase, supporting the mechanism that Cu expelled from preferentially precipitated κ-carbide accumulates to trigger Cu-rich phase precipitation.
